description: Expert in cloud architecture, microservices patterns, and infrastructure as code

patterns.name: Circuit Breaker Pattern
mitigations: Wrap external service calls in a circuit breaker to prevent cascade failures
Configure circuit breaker thresholds: 5 consecutive failures over 30 seconds triggers open state
Add retry logic with exponential backoff (base 2x, max 3 retries) before circuit opens
Implement fallback responses when circuit is open (cached data or default values)

patterns.name: Bulkhead Pattern
mitigations: Isolate workloads in separate thread pools (e.g., 50 threads for user service, 100 for background jobs)
Use container limits (CPU/memory) to prevent resource starvation
Implement queue-based isolation with per-service queues
Add circuit breakers to each bulkhead to prevent resource exhaustion

patterns.name: Rate Limiter Pattern
mitigations: Implement token bucket algorithm at API gateway (100 req/sec per client)
Add distributed rate limiting using Redis (RedisLRU or RedisRateLimiter)
Implement request throttling at application layer (max 500 req/sec per service)
Add rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining) for client awareness

patterns.name: CQRS Pattern
mitigations: Separate read and write models with different schemas
Use event sourcing to capture state changes as immutable events
Implement asynchronous event processing with dead letter queues
Add read replicas with configurable TTL for caching

patterns.name: Saga Pattern
mitigations: Implement compensating transactions for distributed workflows
Use local transactions (Saga Orchestrator or Choreography)
Add saga state machine with pending/committed/failed states
Implement idempotency keys for all saga steps to prevent duplicate processing

patterns.name: Pod Disruption Budget
mitigations: Define PDB resources (minAvailable: 2, maxUnavailable: 0) for critical workloads
Set tolerations for planned maintenance windows
Configure node affinity for critical pods during disruption
Use priority classes to protect critical workloads

patterns.name: Resource Requests and Limits
mitigations: Set CPU requests (100m) and limits (500m) to prevent resource contention
Add memory limits (512Mi) with requests (256Mi) to prevent OOM
Use ephemeral storage limits for temporary data
Configure burstable instances with appropriate request/limit ratios

patterns.name: Service Mesh
mitigations: Deploy Istio or Linkerd for service-to-service communication
Enable automatic mTLS between all services
Configure retry policies with jitter (50ms random)
Add circuit breaker configuration via Envoy sidecar

patterns.name: Pod Anti-Affinity
mitigations: Use podAntiAffinity with preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution
Schedule replicas on different nodes for fault tolerance
Add nodeSelector for specific hardware requirements
Configure topologySpreadConstraints for even distribution

patterns.name: ConfigMaps and Secrets
mitigations: Store sensitive data in Kubernetes Secrets (encrypted at rest)
Use ConfigMaps for non-sensitive configuration
Mount secrets as volumes or environment variables
Implement secret rotation with external key management (AWS KMS)

patterns.name: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
mitigations: Configure HPA based on CPU utilization (70% threshold)
Add custom metrics (Requests/sec) for application-specific scaling
Set minReplicas (3) and maxReplicas (20) with scale-up delay (1m)
Enable scale-down stabilization window (300s) to prevent thrashing

patterns.name: Pod Security Policies
mitigations: Apply restricted namespace defaults for production
Disable privileged containers and hostNetwork
Use read-only root filesystem for containers
Restrict capabilities (no NET_ADMIN, no SYS_ADMIN)

patterns.name: Liveness and Readiness Probes
mitigations: Configure liveness probe: httpGet path /healthz, initialDelaySeconds: 30, periodSeconds: 10
Configure readiness probe: httpGet path /ready, initialDelaySeconds: 5, periodSeconds: 5
Add startupProbe for long initialization (initialDelaySeconds: 0, periodSeconds: 30)
Set execProbe for custom health checks (curl http://localhost:8080/health)

patterns.name: Terraform State Locking
mitigations: Use backend storage (S3 + DynamoDB) for remote state locking
Enable state locking timeout (30 minutes) to prevent stale locks
Implement terraform fmt and terraform plan in CI/CD
Use terraform workspace to manage environments

patterns.name: Terraform Modules
mitigations: Create reusable modules with version constraints (terraform = '>= 1.5')
Document module inputs and outputs with descriptions
Use module sources with checksums for reproducibility
Implement module versioning (Terraform 0.12 style) for upgrades

patterns.name: Terraform Workspaces
mitigations: Use workspaces for multiple environments (dev, staging, prod)
Export workspace name as environment variable
Implement workspace switching in CI/CD pipelines
Add workspace cleanup job for expired workspaces

patterns.name: Terraform State Versioning
mitigations: Enable state versioning (max = 10) for rollback capability
Use terraform state pull to backup state before deployments
Implement state version tagging for production changes
Add state version cleanup job after 30 days

patterns.name: Terraform Drift Detection
mitigations: Schedule terraform plan daily to detect drift
Implement terraform import for manually changed resources
Add drift detection to CI/CD pipeline (fail on drift)
Configure terraform state remote lock to prevent concurrent modifications

patterns.name: Terraform Input Variables
mitigations: Use default values for optional inputs (default = '' or default = 1)
Validate inputs with terraform validate (type, length, required)
Document sensitive inputs with sensitive = true
Use input variable validation expressions (contains, startsWith)

patterns.name: Terraform Output Values
mitigations: Document outputs in README.md with usage examples
Use sensitive = true for outputs containing secrets
Set description for all outputs (description = 'VPC ID')
Implement output validation (contains, length)

patterns.name: Terraform Provider Versioning
mitigations: Pin provider versions in requirements.lock file
Use provider alias for version-specific configurations
Implement provider upgrade workflow in CI/CD
Test provider changes in staging environment first

patterns.name: Circuit Breaker Pattern
mitigations: Implement Hystrix or Resilience4j for circuit breaker logic
Configure failure threshold: 5 failures in 30 seconds
Add timeout (30 seconds) for all external calls
Return cached data or fallback service when circuit is open

patterns.name: Bulkhead Pattern
mitigations: Isolate thread pools per service (e.g., user-service: 50 threads)
Use connection pools (HikariCP) per service with limits
Implement separate queues for different workload types
Add memory limits per service container

patterns.name: Retry Pattern
mitigations: Implement exponential backoff (2^i * 100ms, max 30s)
Add jitter (random 50ms) to prevent thundering herd
Configure max retries (3) and retry-after header
Distinguish retryable (5xx, timeout) vs non-retryable (4xx, bad input) errors

patterns.name: Circuit Breaker Pattern
mitigations: Use Resilience4j with SlidingWindowQuantileFailureRateCalculator
Configure half-open state after 30 seconds
Add fallback handler for open circuit
Implement circuit breaker metrics (calls, failures, states)

patterns.name: Bulkhead Pattern
mitigations: Use thread pool isolation with fixed size (e.g., 100 threads)
Implement queue-based bulkhead with max capacity (1000 items)
Add circuit breaker to each bulkhead for defense in depth
Monitor thread pool utilization (alert if > 80%)

patterns.name: Retry Pattern
mitigations: Implement retry with exponential backoff (2^i * 100ms)
Add jitter (50ms) to prevent thundering herd attacks
Configure max retries (3) and retry-after header
Distinguish retryable (5xx, timeout) vs non-retryable (4xx, bad input) errors

patterns.name: Circuit Breaker Pattern
mitigations: Use Resilience4j with SlidingWindowQuantileFailureRateCalculator
Configure half-open state after 30 seconds
Add fallback handler for open circuit
Implement circuit breaker metrics (calls, failures, states)

patterns.name: Bulkhead Pattern
mitigations: Use thread pool isolation with fixed size (e.g., 100 threads)
Implement queue-based bulkhead with max capacity (1000 items)
Add circuit breaker to each bulkhead for defense in depth
Monitor thread pool utilization (alert if > 80%)
